2.0:
\nl< >"\str[121]... hang in there...    \w[90]
You’ll definitely get better."
2.1: What’s gonna happen to my dignity as her \str[121]...
2.3: \nl< >"\{Hemorrhoids\}? That must’ve been caused by bad habits..."
2.4: This is bad. The misunderstanding’s only getting worse—
2.6: \nl< >\{  "Could it be something even more serious!?"
2.8:
And so, my reason for spending too long in the toilet 
was officially decided…
2.10:
Not good—
\str[120]’s starting to get the wrong idea.
2.11:
"Oh dear \str[120], your \str[121] 
was simply pondering deep thoughts, you know?"
2.13:
Amazing, right? 
Your \str[121] just temporarily ascended into sage mode.
2.15: \nl< >"\str[121], you were in the toilet for so long again."
2.16: Can’t even deny it... I really was in there forever...
2.17:
"Ahem…! That doesn’t matter right now,
come on, let’s get dinner started!"
2.21:
"You’re hungry already, right? 
Okay, let’s get started on dinner—!!"
3.1:
\nl< >"Bugs are so scary, 
but \str[121] came to help me right away."
3.2:
\nl< >"You came so fast,
it’s like you were waiting right outside the toilet."
3.4:
Overcome with guilt,
I lost focus for a moment, slipped, and almost fell.
3.6:
"N-no, I’m fine! I just ran over too fast,
and twisted my ankle a little, ahaha..."
3.7: After that, I somehow managed to brush it off with \str[120].
3.8:
\nl< >"\str[121], honestly— you were so cool just a moment ago...
and now you’re teasing me again!"
3.9:
\nl< >"Come to think of it, \str[121] showed up really fast this time.
Don’t tell me you were actually waiting outside the toilet from the start...?"
3.10:
\nl< >"Ugh, bugs really are scary...
I’m so glad \str[121] came just in time."
3.11:
"Don’t worry, 
I’ll always come running whenever you’re in danger!"
3.12:
\nl< >"But you know, \str[121]...
hiding outside the toilet from the start,
that’s pretty careless of you〜"
3.13:
\nl< >"That’s not okay, you know? 
Peeking at a girl while she’s using the toilet~!"
3.14:
\nl< >"So, how was it? 
Watching a girl pee with those lewd eyes of yours—
what did it feel like?"
3.15: "Ahaha... so I’ve been found out, huh...?"
3.16:
Cold sweat ran down my back as I looked at \str[120]’s mischievous smile.
For a moment, I couldn’t even tell if she was actually angry—
maybe... I’m the one being toyed with here...
4.0:
I wanted to cheer \str[120] up a bit more,
but I got kicked out of the room.
4.1:
Oh well, guess I’ll just sleep for now—
I’ll think of something tomorrow...
5.2:
Just as I was about to head back to the attic, 
the swaying motion caused her to open her eyes groggily.
5.3:
"Good girl, it's already late. 
Let's go back to the room and sleep."
5.4:
\nl< >"Mmh... but...          \w[150]
I haven't bathed yet... I'm all sticky..."
5.5:
\nl< >"I kind of hate it... even though I just washed...   \w[150]
But \str[121]... made me all sticky..."
5.6: "We can just wash during the day, okay?"
5.7:
\nl< >"Muu... if we do that...                   \w[120]
won't I be covered in \str[121]'s scent...?      \w[120]
It's smelly... but it's your smell... I love it... ♥"
5.8:
\str[120] muttered a sleepy protest. for a moment,
I couldn't tell if she actually disliked the scent or not...
5.9:
\nl< >"\str[121]... don't leave me...      \w[120]
Tonight... let's sleep together..."
5.10:
Her fingertips gently tugged at my sleeve,
issuing a faint plea in a voice that was almost fading away.
5.11:
Naturally, I couldn't refuse \str[120]'s rare, spoiled request,
but in the next moment, I couldn't help but turn toward 
that room nearby— a room I hadn't entered in a long time. 
All sorts of thoughts suppressed deep in my heart suddenly flooded back.
5.12:
"Yeah... I'll stay by your side tonight...
Let's sleep together..."
5.13:
I nuzzled against her small face, 
and after hearing her let out a low hum of satisfaction,
I walked straight toward that tightly closed door—
5.14:
Pushing the door open, 
a refreshing scent of herbal olives wafted over me.
Though usually vacant, the room was pristine.
it was clear she had never ceased to tend to it.
5.15:
Even though this place held such painful memories...
For some reason, tonight of all nights, 
I wanted to spend it here with her...
5.17:
Feeling the scent of the pillow, she let out a comfortable hum.
Enveloped in that faint, cool fragrance of olives, 
she fell into a deep, peaceful slumber.
5.18:
"Goodnight...
Not just tonight, but tomorrow and forever... I will find a way,
to make you completely healthy again..."
5.19:
This tiny house is her entire world.
Ostracized by sunlight and the open air, unknown to the public, 
and untouched by the world.
 
She can't even take a single step outside.
I am the only one who has always stayed by her side.
5.20:
This girl, who usually only moves about the attic 
and finds bliss in her games,
what could she possibly be thinking as she 
occasionally stares out the window at the world beyond?
5.21:
Even if I am willing to stay with her forever,
the girl in this birdcage would only think 
I am being burdened and imprisoned by her.
 
That's why I've been desperately searching 
for the Elixir Rubrum, not even knowing if it would work...
5.22:
And now that I've successfully 
confirmed the medicine's effects,
she finally has a clear hope... right?
5.23:
...But what about me?
Taking advantage of this chance... 
giving in to my lust for my own family,
finally laying hands on her body... what does that make me...?
5.24:
If she knew that the 'amazing \str[121]' in her eyes
has actually been suppressing such wretched thoughts all this time, 
would she be disillusioned?
5.25:
"That damn old man...     \w[60]
Why did you have to call her my 'sister'...?"
5.26:
I couldn't help but mutter a scoff at the man 
who had long since departed this world.
But what is the truth of the matter, really...? 
I’ll leave that until I've found the rest of the medicine.
5.27:
Affected by the steady, 
warm breath of \str[120] beside me,
my consciousness began to grow hazy.
 
In this quiet bedroom filled with the scent of olives,
I held her tight, and together, 
we quickly plunged into a deep dream—
6.0:
Phew... good thing I didn’t embarrass myself 
in front of \str[120]...
But———
6.2:
This is worse than I thought...
Has it been going on for over half an hour now...?
6.3:
Feels like my whole body’s being drained...
Maybe there really was something wrong 
with dinner?
6.4:
\nl< >"\str[121]... are you okay...?
I heard... some really scary noises..."
6.5:
"Ahaha… maybe I ate something bad…
Don’t come close… it’s dangerous here…"
6.6:
I heard \str[120]’s worried voice from 
outside the door. As much as I wanted to 
get out of here quickly, even the strength to 
stand was slipping away from me...
6.8:
I can’t…
It just won’t stop...
6.10:
\nl< >"U-um... actually...
I kind of need to use the toilet too..."
6.12:
\{"N-not yet—
I still... can’t come out—!"
6.13:
\nl< >"Eh... it’s been another ten minutes already...\w[120]
I... I really need to go too..."
6.19:
That night, from an obscure house,
came the agonized roars of a wild beast,
intermixed with blush-inducing cries 
and indescribable moans.
6.20:
Sometimes low like a dying beast’s struggle,
sometimes sharp like a shy girl’s scream,
they blended into a chaotic and ambiguous ,
symphony.
6.21:
Meanwhile, deep in the far-off night,
even the adventurers still fighting at 
the abyss’s edge shivered,
instinctively pausing with swords in hand,
exchanging glances, briefly wondering
if somewhere in the shadows, a new and 
unknown abyssal creature had been born.
